THE COURT HAVING REVIEWED THE PLEADINGS AND HAVING HEARD ORAL ARGUMENT RULES AS FOLLOWS: THE COURT CONSTRUES THE MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION AS A MOTION TO SET ASIDE THE DISMISSAL UNDER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 473(B) AND/OR THE COURT'S EQUITABLE POWERS, AND HEREBY GRANTS THE MOTION. DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-29-2019 Continued To Oct-10-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Plaintiff JOHANNA WELTY (Tel: 925-708-4505) appearing in pro per;
Atty. DAMON M. THURSTON, ESQ. of Law Offices of Rankin, Sproat, Mires, Reynolds, Shuey & Mintz APC (Tel: 510-465-3922) appearing for defendant Craig Morrison.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT CRAIG MORRISON'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Matter discussed by the Court and the parties. The Court vacates its tentative ruling and continues the hearing on defendant Craig Morrison's Demurrer to Plaintiff's Second Amended Complaint to October 10, 2019 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302, Law & Motion. The Court orders the parties to meet and confer either in person or via telephone by September 13, 2019. Plaintiff indicates a preference for a meet and confer in person which defense counsel is amenable to so long as the meet and confer takes place at his law office. If there is no agreement after the meet and confer, the Court orders that any opposition by the plaintiff to the demurrer must be filed by September 27, 2019 and that plaintiff must deliver courtesy copies of any opposition papers directly to Dept. 302. The Court orders that any reply by the defendant is to be filed by the statutory deadline. The Court reminds the parties that a tentative ruling on the demurrer will be posted by the court online the court day prior to the October 10th hearing date.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano, CSR#8600, e-mail: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)

Plaintiff Johanna Welty'S Motion For Court To Reconsider April 2, 2019 Dismissal (Tentative Ruling Only Part 2 Of 2)
Off Calendar May-30-2019 Off Calendar

Plaintiff Johanna Welty'S Motion For Court To Reconsider April 2, 2019 Dismissal And Seventh Declaration Of Johanna Welty, Plaintiff Pro Per, Respectfully Submitted To The Superior Court Of California, County Of San Francisco (Case Management Dept 601/ Department 302) In Response To April 2, 2019 Order Granting Motion For Dismissal Filed By Defendant Craig W. Morrison
Off Calendar May-30-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Johanna Welty, 925-708-4505 Plaintiff in Pro Per.
Damon Thurston, Esq. 510-465-3622 of Rankin Sproat Mires Reynolds Shuey & Mintz for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court announces the tentative ruling and the reasons for it. The Court finds no adequate complaint on file and asks why Plaintiff has not complied with the Court's orders. Plaintiff argues. Defendant responds. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Plaintiff Johanna Welty'S Motion For Court To Reconsider April 2, 2019 Dismissal And Seventh Declaration Of Johanna Welty, Plaintiff Pro Per, Respectfully Submitted To The Superior Court Of California, County Of San Francisco (Case Management Dept 601/ Department 302) In Response To April 2, 2019 Order Granting Motion For Dismissal Filed By Defendant Craig W. Morrison.

The Court construes the motion for reconsideration as a motion to set aside the dismissal under Code of Civil Procedure section 473(b) and/or the Court's equitable powers, and hereby grants the motion. Plaintiff shall file and serve her proposed second amended complaint on May 31, 2019.(as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-17-2019 Off Calendar

MOTION TO DISMISS
Off Calendar Apr-02-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Damon M. Thurston, Esq., of Rankin, Sproat et al, appearing for Defendant, Craig Morrison;
No other appearance.

No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CRAIG MORRISON MOTION TO DISMISS

Defendant Craig W. Morrison's motion to dismiss the action pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure 581(f)(2) is granted. Plaintiff failed to comply with the Court's November 29, 2018 Order, which granted her leave to file a second amended complaint by January 31, 2019, and has not filed any opposition to the motion.

The Court is aware of Ms. Welty's "Fifth Declaration," filed with the Court on March 21, 2019. That declaration was presented for filing in the Court's case management department in response to a February 21, 2019 order to show cause, and was not filed in response to this motion. The physician's letter attached to the declaration, dated January 18, 2019, states only Ms. Welty "should be given some extra time to complete her work duties for this month," while she was being treated for a condition causing her visual impairment. Ms. Welty did not seek a further extension of time to file a second amended complaint, nor has she shown good cause for such an extension.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: W. Trupek, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
